classification_rationale: afi-mint is the off-chain minting *coordination* spine of the reference stack, not a protocol-normative source. package.json:3 "AFI signal validation and token minting coordination"; AGENTS.md:3 "coordinates signal-driven token minting... but does NOT contain token contracts or on-chain economics logic"; AGENTS.md:79 "afi-mint = minting coordination (off-chain logic...)". It implements one concrete spine: a Snapshot-governance appeal pipeline (ValidatorDaemon, SignalStateManager) and an emissions/goldpaper data provider, calling an interface IMintCoordinatorContract.mintForSignal() (MintExecutor.ts:32-37). The on-chain contracts here are empty stubs (contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5 "// Stub for minting logic"), and the canonical receipt/emissions definitions live elsewhere (afi-token contracts, afi-math). It is a working reference implementation, but a few artifacts (mint_receipt_schema.json, the signalId/epoch/beneficiary/receiptId/extraData breadcrumb shape) carry near-normative weight for on-chain<->off-chain linkage.
primary_purpose: Off-chain coordination of signal-driven AFI token minting: an appeal-based validator pipeline (auto qualify/reject by decay score, challenge window, Snapshot dispute voting) that finalizes signals and invokes an on-chain AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal() to emit a per-signal mint breadcrumb (signalId, epoch, beneficiary, receiptId).

reference_only_assumptions.quote: afi-reactor = signal orchestration (DAG pipeline, signal scoring); afi-mint = signal consumption ... Dependency direction: afi-mint -> afi-reactor (never reverse)
Implements IMintDataProvider using the canonical AFI emissions schedule ... Based on the goldpaper formula: ΔAFIᵢ = clamp(B(t) × Qᵢ × Nᵢ × R_val,i × E_epoch, ...)

contradictions.title: Two divergent SignalValidatorState state machines (Zod schema vs TS types)
contradictions.detail: The canonical TS state machine in src/orchestrator/types.ts uses kinds pending/qualified/rejected/challenge_window/contested/dispute_resolved/finalized/minted/rejected_final, while the Zod validation schema in schemas/SignalValidatorState.schema.ts uses a completely different, older set: pending/decay_pass/challenge_open/voting_complete/minted/rejected. An external validator using the published @afi-protocol/afi-mint/schemas Zod schema to validate state records would reject every record produced by the actual orchestrator. This breaks interoperability of the signal lifecycle dialect.

contradictions.title: Reputation/governance can override deterministic mint outcome and amount
contradictions.detail: North-star rule 5 says reputation/registries/governance influence SELECTION & ALLOCATION only and MUST NOT override deterministic scoring or protocol finality. Here a Snapshot governance vote (DisputeResolver) can OVERTURN the validator's deterministic decay-score decision (qualified->reject / rejected->mint), directly flipping mint finality; and per-signal token amount is multiplied by reputationWeight and a governance-controlled epochPulseFactor. This conflates governance with deterministic scoring/finality.
contradictions.evidence: src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:284-286 'if (challengeSucceeded) { finalDecision = state.validatorDecision === \'qualified\' ? \'reject\' : \'mint\'; }'; src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:270-281 'R = metadata.reputationWeight ... adjustedAmount = baseAmount * this.config.epochPulseFactor'

contradictions.title: Non-deterministic constructs in a replay-critical mint path
contradictions.detail: Reference spine claims deterministic audit/replay (README.md:59 'deterministic audit trails'), but mint amount and state records embed wall-clock time (Date.now/new Date().toISOString throughout), and the emissions calc depends on mutable epoch-shared state (getEpochMintedAmount/getEpochTotalWeight) plus floating-point Math.exp/division before BigInt floor. Same inputs do not guarantee same scored/minted outputs across stacks unless time and epoch accumulation are snapshotted, which is not enforced here.

contradictions.title: On-chain commitment presented as full mint engine but contracts are empty stubs
contradictions.detail: README.md:44 advertises 'Solidity Contracts for On-Chain Mint Management' and contracts/ lists ChallengeRegistry/MintManager/ThresholdRules, yet all three are empty stub contracts. The actual on-chain commitment plane (mint events, supply caps, receipts) lives in afi-token. This matches the 'BASE = full ledger (vision) vs receipt-only (reality)' tension and risks readers treating these stubs as the commitment layer.
contradictions.evidence: contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5 'contract MintManager { // Stub for minting logic }'; contracts/ThresholdRules.sol:4 '// Stub for DAO-approved minting thresholds'; contracts/ChallengeRegistry.sol:4 '// Stub for handling signal mint challenges'

contradictions.title: Single-beneficiary mint model, no econ splits / gauge
contradictions.detail: MintRequest carries exactly one beneficiary and one tokenAmount per signal; there is no split/gauge/multi-beneficiary distribution anywhere in the repo. This is consistent with the known 'single beneficiary mint vs econ splits/gauge' tension and means the reference coordinator cannot express revenue/emission splits described in broader econ vision.
contradictions.evidence: src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:19-27 'interface MintRequest { beneficiary: string; tokenAmount: bigint; ... }'; grep for split|gauge|beneficiaries returned no logic hits

contradictions.title: Stale root-level placeholder files and broken CLI references
contradictions.detail: Root contains legacy single-line placeholders (mint_trigger.ts, challenge_window.ts, threshold_checker.ts) and duplicate CLI/script stubs that predate the src/ implementation. cli/simulate-mint.ts calls runMintFlow() with no args, but mint/mint.ts exports runMintFlow(daemon: ValidatorDaemon) requiring a daemon -> the simulate CLI cannot run. .aider.conf.json declares repo_name 'afi-core' and references nonexistent dirs (runtime/, mentor/, signal_schema_test/).
contradictions.evidence: cli/simulate-mint.ts:2-3 'import { runMintFlow } from \'../mint/mint\'; runMintFlow();' vs mint/mint.ts:14 'export async function runMintFlow(daemon: ValidatorDaemon)'; .aider.conf.json:31 '"repo_name": "afi-core"'; threshold_checker.ts:1 '// Placeholder for score threshold validation logic.'

upstream: afi-math (emissions schedule inlined 'to avoid circular dependency', canonical source afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts)
downstream: afi-token (afi-mint invokes IMintCoordinatorContract.mintForSignal on-chain; AGENTS.md dependency afi-mint -> afi-token)

notable_findings.title: Mint breadcrumb shape matches 'receipt, not full payload' on-chain reality
notable_findings.evidence: src/orchestrator/MintExecutor.ts:19-27 'interface MintRequest { beneficiary; tokenAmount; receiptId; receiptAmount; signalId; epoch; extraData }' and :146 'receiptAmount: 1n, ... extraData: \'0x\''
notable_findings.recommendation: Confirm this MintRequest is the canonical on-chain anchor shape and that extraData is the slot for future content/payload hashes; cross-check against afi-token AFIMintCoordinator.mintForSignal signature. Promote to spec if it is the interoperability contract.

notable_findings.title: Mint receipt schema is the audit/replay anchor but is minimal
notable_findings.evidence: codex/mint_receipt_schema.json:6-13 required ['signal_id','score','mint_amount','validator_id','challenge_status','timestamp'] (no epoch, no beneficiary, no content/payload hash, no pinned-rule/version fields)
notable_findings.recommendation: The receipt lacks epoch, beneficiary, and any pinned-transform/version or content-hash fields needed for challenge-ready replay. Align it with MintRequest and add determinism-anchoring fields (epoch, beneficiary, rulesetVersion, payloadHash).

notable_findings.title: Snapshot governance vote determines mint finality (selection vs finality boundary)
notable_findings.recommendation: Document whether governance overturning a deterministic validator decision is intended protocol behavior. Per north-star rule 5 governance must not override deterministic scoring/finality; clarify this as appeal-of-record vs finality override.

notable_findings.title: TSSD/vault touched only via interface; no DB engine assumed (good for portability)
notable_findings.evidence: src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:162-166 'Interface for fetching signal metadata from TSSD vault. interface ISignalMetadataFetcher { getMetadata(...) }'; src/orchestrator/SignalStateManager.ts:57-62 'interface ISignalStateStore' with only InMemory impl
notable_findings.recommendation: Positive: persistence is abstracted behind ISignalStateStore/ISignalMetadataFetcher with no Mongo/Postgres hardcoding. Note as evidence that afi-mint does NOT exhibit Mongo tunnel vision.

notable_findings.title: No USS/CPJ ingest-dialect validation present
notable_findings.evidence: grep for usignal|USS|cpj|CPJ across repo returned zero hits
notable_findings.recommendation: Expected: afi-mint consumes already-scored signals (analyst score) not raw USS/CPJ; ingest-dialect validation belongs to afi-gateway/afi-core. No action.

notable_findings.title: Emissions/goldpaper math inlined and duplicated from afi-math
notable_findings.evidence: src/adapters/EmissionsMintDataProvider.ts:18-21 'Inlined from afi-math to avoid circular dependency issues. Canonical source: afi-math/src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts'; cap 86_000_000_000n at :51
notable_findings.recommendation: Duplicated emissions constants/algorithm risk drift from the canonical afi-math source, undermining deterministic replay. Replace the inlined copy with a versioned import or pin a shared emissions ruleset version recorded in the receipt.

notable_findings.title: BigQuery/Kafka/warehouse and Postgres/Timescale/Influx entirely absent
notable_findings.recommendation: Consistent with afi-mint being commitment-coordination, not the analytics/market or evidence-vault plane. No action for this repo; multi-engine concern lives in vault.schema.json / afi-infra.

notable_findings.title: reputation_bridge is an unimplemented placeholder that explicitly defers to canonical invariants
notable_findings.evidence: src/reputation_bridge.ts:95-99 'TODO: implement reputation-weighted emission logic ... return []'; :8 'does NOT modify vault finality or the Universal Weighting Rule (UWR)'
notable_findings.recommendation: Good guardrail comments; ensure the eventual implementation imports canonical reputation types rather than re-declaring 'Minimal' views (types.ts:18-39 TODO 'Replace with a direct import').

notable_findings.title: DEFAULT_VALIDATOR_CONFIG hardcodes economic/governance parameters in code
notable_findings.evidence: src/orchestrator/types.ts:199-217 minDecayScoreThreshold 0.5, challengeSuccessThreshold 0.66, minChallengeStake '100000000000000000000', challengeSlashRate 0.5, snapshotSpaceId 'afi.eth'
notable_findings.recommendation: AGENTS.md:135 forbids hardcoding minting parameters ('use config from afi-config'). These defaults are reasonable for a reference impl but should be clearly marked reference-only and sourced from afi-config in production paths.

notable_findings.title: Root .ts placeholders and CLI/script duplication are stale
notable_findings.evidence: mint_trigger.ts:1 '// Placeholder for mint event trigger to xERC20-compatible cross-chain contract.'; scripts/mint_trigger.ts:1 '// CLI hook for triggering a mint event'; cli/mint_trigger.ts:2 'Mint trigger CLI stub'
notable_findings.recommendation: Tag stale: remove or consolidate the root-level placeholder .ts files and the three near-empty CLI/script trigger stubs that are superseded by src/orchestrator + mint/mint.ts.
